name: Malagon, Zarzalejo, Silla de Felipe II y Monasterio -- San Lorenzo de El Escorial
name_original: Malagón, Zarzalejo, Silla de Felipe II y Monasterio — San Lorenzo de El Escorial
region_intro: Mountain biking around San Lorenzo De El Escorial is characterized by its location within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, offering diverse natural features. The terrain includes dense pine forests like Pinar de Abantos, river valleys, and impressive granite formations. This region provides varied landscapes for mountain bikers, from challenging mountain ascents to routes through protected natural areas. Best mountain bike trails around San Lorenzo De El Escorial The most popular mountain bike trail is Malagon, Zarzalejo, Silla de Felipe II y Monasterio -- San Lorenzo de El Escorial, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Royal Monastery and the Guadarrama Valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mount Abantos and Peguerinos Reservoir Loop, a difficult 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route traverses the Pinar de Abantos and provides scenic views of the Peguerinos Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Las Zetas de Abantos – Puerto de Malagón loop from Hospital de El Escorial, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail leading through the slopes of Mount Abantos, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around San Lorenzo De El Escorial is defined by the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, dense pine forests, and granite rock formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Lorenzo De El Escorial are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Nearly 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore San Lorenzo De El Escorial's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find around San Lorenzo de El Escorial?
A: The region offers a diverse network of mountain bike trails, ranging from easy routes with minimal elevation gain to challenging, technical singletrack. You'll find paths through dense pine forests like Pinar de Abantos, river valleys, and areas with impressive granite formations, all within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in San Lorenzo de El Escorial?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options for less experienced riders. The area includes trails with easier difficulty levels, perfect for those looking for a more relaxed ride or a family outing. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Oficios del Campillo – Guadarrama River Bridge loop from San Lorenzo de El Escorial&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers a good balance for various skill levels.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can expect to see on the trails?
A: Mountain biking in this region offers stunning natural beauty. You can expect to traverse areas like the Pinar de Abantos, a prominent pine forest, and enjoy views of the Valmayor Reservoir. A particularly famous viewpoint is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6491989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chair of Philip II (Silla de Felipe II)&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking panoramic views of the Royal Monastery and the Guadarrama Valley. Many routes also pass through areas with unique granite formations.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in this area?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance, elevation, and your riding pace. For instance, a challenging route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Concha – Las Zetas de Abantos loop from Ctra. M600 - Cº Calzada del Campillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46.6 km long, typically takes around 3 hours 52 minutes. Shorter, easier routes will naturally take less time.

Q: What kind of elevation changes should I expect on mountain bike routes here?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama terrain means you'll encounter significant elevation changes, especially on difficult routes. Many challenging trails feature over 700 meters of ascent, with some exceeding 1000 meters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962658714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Abantos and Peguerinos Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Moderate routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Oficios del Campillo – Guadarrama River Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt;, have more manageable ascents around 270 meters.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the mountain biking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The iconic Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is often visible from various viewpoints along the trails, blending historical grandeur with natural splendor. You can also find mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1771270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Malagón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4220056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del León&lt;/a&gt;, which have historical significance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in San Lorenzo de El Escorial?
A: The mountain biking trails around San Lorenzo de El Escorial are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from nearly 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the extensive network of paths that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for technical or advanced mountain biking?
A: Yes, the region is well-known for its challenging terrain. Areas around Mount Abantos, for example, feature routes with significant elevation gain and technical sections. The 'Vera Trail' is specifically highlighted for enduro mountain biking, known for its 'flow' and scenic surroundings, appealing to advanced riders seeking a thrilling experience.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in San Lorenzo de El Escorial?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter riding is possible, but higher elevations may experience snow and colder conditions, requiring appropriate gear and caution.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Many of the popular mountain bike routes in San Lorenzo de El Escorial are loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Concha – Las Zetas de Abantos loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Oficios del Campillo – Guadarrama River Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the San Lorenzo de El Escorial area?
A: There are numerous mountain bike trails in and around San Lorenzo de El Escorial. Komoot alone lists 13 distinct routes, with a total of 2 easy, 1 moderate, and 10 difficult trails. Other platforms indicate hundreds of trails, offering extensive options for mountain biking, e-biking, and gravel biking.
tips: Halfway up to Abantos, to the right we have a small wall from which we have spectacular views of the Monastery of El Escorial. Obligatory stop
Despite the sign, the water is very good to the point that people from the town come to collect it

This mountain pass is of great importance in the Spanish cycling scene. A large number of editions of the Tour of Spain have passed through the port of Malagón and the port has been the end of the stage on many occasions.
Very TOP track facing GRAVEL. It is enjoyed like a dwarf in the sense of the title. Maybe the other way around costs something more 😀😉🤙🏻
A classic for bikers and road cycling and in any case, the bar is a good place to refresh ourselves

In this small town in the Sierra Oesta was the quarry from which the granite was extracted to build the Monastery of El Escorial. It is located at the foot of Machota Alta, better known as Pico del Fraile.

From this natural observatory the monarch contemplated the development of the works of the Monastery.
Some experts assure that these granite seats, for the king and two companions, are the remains of a thousand-year-old Celtiberian altar. From here you can perceive that the Monastery has the shape of an inverted grill, in homage to San Lorenzo, who was martyred in one of them back in the year 258, since it is located on a small hill from which the Guadarrama Valley and the Sierra de Navacerrada.

A visit that you cannot miss! The monastery has been a world heritage site since 1984 and is one of the best ways to appreciate and understand the "golden" era of Spain reigned by Philip II.

You can consult the official website for visiting hours and purchase tickets

https://www.patrimonionacional.es/visita/real-monasterio-de-san-lorenzo-de-el-escorial
